videos.title: New Claude Opus 4.8: 15 Things You May’ve Missed
videos.summary: This video analyzes the release of Claude Opus 4.8, highlighting 15 key aspects from its 244-page system card, private testing, and interviews. It covers the model's performance, including benchmarks, and new features like Fast Mode and Dynamic Workflows. The analysis also delves into critical areas such as model honesty, uncertainty flagging, model welfare, cyber comparability, and misalignment concerns, providing insights into what the release signifies for the AI landscape.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Two Rival Bets on AGI: Google I/O Highlights
videos.summary: This video analyzes Google's AI strategy, contrasting it with approaches from OpenAI and Anthropic, particularly in the context of AGI development. It highlights the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash, discussing its implications for the near-term future of AI and its cost-effectiveness. The video also covers agentic task search, a new paper on AI negation, and Andrej Karpathy's recent activities, drawing insights from Google I/O and various AI lab interviews.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: GPT 5.5 Arrives, DeepSeek V4 Drops, and the Compute War Intensifies
videos.summary: This video analyzes the release of GPT 5.5, including comparisons and system card details, and introduces DeepSeek V4 with paper highlights and its implications for the 'compute war'. It also explores recursive self-improvement, an experiment with GPT Image 2 called 'VibeCode', and discusses the era of scarce compute. Various benchmarks like SWE Bench Pro and Omniscience are mentioned, alongside insights into Claude's code limitations and ChatGPT's application for clinicians.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Claude Opus 4.7 - A New Frontier, in Performance … and Drama
videos.summary: This video analyzes the release of Claude Opus 4.7, examining its performance against benchmarks where it reportedly trails Gemini but leads GPT 5.4. It covers Anthropic's acknowledged compute limitations affecting the model's capability and user dissatisfaction with recent code updates. The discussion also explores market share dynamics, specific "Mythos" findings, and the long-standing rivalry between OpenAI and Anthropic, particularly regarding their differing philosophies on code development. The video touches on the impact of large-scale AI projects and associated industry drama.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Claude Mythos: Highlights from 244-page Release
videos.summary: The video analyzes the new AI model, Claude Mythos, based on its 244-page system card. It covers the model's internal release and availability, general capabilities, potential for self-improvement, and safety decisions. The discussion also touches on its coding abilities, alignment, awareness, GUI for agents, and the issue of hallucinations, drawing parallels to the movie 'Her'. The creator's description of the model as 'terrifying' is also highlighted.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Two AI Models Set to “stir government urgency”, But Will This Challenge Undo Them?
videos.summary: This video discusses exclusive reports on OpenAI's new "Spud" model and an upcoming Anthropic model, both expected to prompt government urgency regarding AI. It also analyzes the newly launched ARC-AGI-3 benchmark, highlighting its extreme difficulty and unique scoring metrics. The video explores what these developments, particularly the benchmark's challenges, signify for the future of AI in 2026, touching upon potential implications for AI governance and the pursuit of AGI.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: What the New ChatGPT 5.4 Means for the World
videos.summary: This video discusses OpenAI's rapid release of GPT 5.4 Thinking, following GPT 5.3 Instant, and its implications for AI progress. It also provides context on the conflict between the Pentagon and Anthropic, including the use of Claude in Iran and corporate governance issues. The video aims to cover AI performance, safety, and the broader impact of these developments on jobs and the future of AI.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Deadline Day for Autonomous AI Weapons & Mass Surveillance
videos.summary: The video discusses the ongoing conflict between Anthropic and the Pentagon regarding the use of AI models like Claude for autonomous weapons and mass surveillance. It explores Anthropic's rejection of Pentagon terms, its existing policies, and the recent dropping of its Responsible Scaling Policy. The video also highlights potential risks associated with Claude agents, referencing new research papers, and mentions a petition related to AI ethics. The narrative covers five twists in the story, emphasizing the ethical and policy dilemmas surrounding AI development and military applications.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro and the Downfall of Benchmarks: Welcome to the Vibe Era of AI
videos.summary: The video discusses the release of Gemini 3.1 Pro and Sonnet 4.6, questioning whether these models represent a new best in AI or signify the downfall of traditional benchmarks as a measure of machine intelligence. It provides a breakdown of Gemini 3.1 Pro, including its performance on benchmarks like ARC-AGI 2 and Simple Bench, and addresses issues like hallucination. The analysis incorporates insights from several research papers and posts, touching upon topics such as post-training dominance, agentic coding, and continual learning, suggesting a shift towards a "Vibe Era of AI" where qualitative assessment might become more prominent.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: The Two Best AI Models/Enemies Just Got Released Simultaneously
videos.summary: The video discusses the simultaneous release of Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT 5.3 Codex, promising a detailed breakdown of reports comparing the two. Key topics include AI self-improvement, their application in knowledge work, agentic behavior, the concept of Claude's 'personhood,' and unexpected misbehavior from Opus 4.6. The analysis aims to determine which model is superior.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Claude AI Co-founder Publishes 4 Big Claims about Near Future: Breakdown
videos.summary: Anthropic's CEO, Dario Amodei, published an essay outlining his vision for AI's near future. He predicts that AI, driven by scaling and recursion, will evolve from coding automation to full engineering automation. Amodei warns of significant economic displacement within 1-2 years and expresses concern about China's potential use of AI for totalitarianism. Additionally, he foresees a shift in understanding AI models as collections of distinct personas rather than monolithic systems.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: How I use LLMs
videos.summary: Andrej Karpathy provides an example-driven, practical walkthrough of how he uses Large Language Models and their related features in his daily life. The video covers basic LLM interactions, understanding different models and pricing, advanced 'thinking models', and various tool uses like internet search, deep research, and Python interpreters. It also explores multimodal capabilities including file uploads, advanced data analysis, code generation with Cursor, audio input/output, image input (OCR) and output (DALL-E), and video input/output (Sora). The discussion extends to LLM memory, custom instructions, and custom GPTs, offering a comprehensive look at the evolving LLM ecosystem.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Deep Dive into LLMs like ChatGPT
videos.summary: This video offers a comprehensive deep dive into Large Language Models (LLMs) like ChatGPT, covering their full training stack from pretraining data and tokenization to neural network internals and inference. It explores post-training techniques such as supervised finetuning and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). The discussion also includes mental models for understanding LLM "psychology," addressing concepts like hallucinations, tool use, knowledge, working memory, and the importance of tokens for LLM "thought." Practical applications and resources for finding and utilizing LLMs are also provided.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Let's reproduce GPT-2 (124M)
videos.summary: This video provides a comprehensive tutorial on reproducing the GPT-2 (124M) model from scratch. It covers the entire process, starting with building the GPT-2 network architecture and implementing its forward pass. Key aspects include optimizing training speed through techniques like mixed precision, Flash Attention, and `torch.compile`, as well as setting up hyperparameters based on the GPT-2 and GPT-3 papers. The tutorial also delves into data loading, distributed data parallel (DDP) training, and evaluating the trained model using the HellaSwag benchmark, concluding with a review of the results and a mention of the `llm.c` project.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Let's build the GPT Tokenizer
videos.summary: This video is a tutorial on building a GPT tokenizer from scratch, explaining its crucial role in Large Language Models (LLMs) for translating between strings and tokens. It delves into the Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) algorithm, Unicode encodings, and the implementation of `encode()` and `decode()` functions. Andrej Karpathy discusses common tokenization-related issues in LLMs, the use of regex patterns, and special tokens. The video also introduces popular libraries like OpenAI's `tiktoken` and Google's `sentencepiece`, touches on multimodal tokenization and prompt compression, and expresses a desire for a future without this stage.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: [1hr Talk] Intro to Large Language Models
videos.summary: Andrej Karpathy delivers an introductory talk on Large Language Models, detailing their core technical components, inference, training, and finetuning into assistants. He explores future directions such as scaling laws, tool use, multimodality, self-improvement, and LLM customization, drawing analogies to operating systems. The presentation also addresses critical security challenges including jailbreaks, prompt injection, and data poisoning, while cautioning against trusting LLM outputs, particularly for memory-based information or mathematical/coding tasks, recommending double-checking.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Let's build GPT: from scratch, in code, spelled out.
topics: Attention is All You Need paper
videos.summary: Andrej Karpathy guides viewers through building a Generatively Pretrained Transformer (GPT) from scratch, drawing inspiration from "Attention is All You Need" and OpenAI's GPT-2/3. The tutorial covers foundational concepts such as tokenization, data loading, and a bigram language model baseline, before diving deep into the transformer architecture. Key components explained include self-attention, multi-headed attention, feedforward layers, residual connections, and layer normalization, all implemented in PyTorch. The video also discusses connections to ChatGPT, pretraining, finetuning, and RLHF, and provides exercises for further learning.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Building makemore Part 5: Building a WaveNet
videos.summary: This video, part of the "Building makemore" series, demonstrates how to construct a convolutional neural network architecture similar to DeepMind's WaveNet (2016). It evolves a previous 2-layer MLP into a deeper, tree-like structure, focusing on practical implementation using PyTorch's `torch.nn` module. The tutorial covers aspects like handling multidimensional tensor shapes, debugging `batchnorm1d`, and the general deep learning development process, including the concept of causal dilated convolutions for more efficient WaveNet implementation.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Building makemore Part 4: Becoming a Backprop Ninja
videos.summary: This video, part of the "Building makemore" series, guides viewers through manually implementing backpropagation for a 2-layer Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) that includes Batch Normalization and cross-entropy loss. It bypasses PyTorch's autograd to provide a deep, intuitive understanding of how gradients flow backward through the computational graph, from the loss function through various layers and the embedding table. The goal is to enhance competence in neural network optimization, debugging, and innovation by understanding the underlying mechanics at a tensor level.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Building makemore Part 3: Activations & Gradients, BatchNorm
videos.summary: This video, part of the "Building makemore" series, explores the internal mechanics of multi-layer perceptrons (MLPs). It scrutinizes the statistics of forward pass activations and backward pass gradients, highlighting issues with improper scaling. The video introduces diagnostic tools for understanding deep network health and explains the fragility of training deep neural nets. It then presents Batch Normalization as a crucial innovation for stabilizing training, discussing its implementation and benefits. The content also touches upon Kaiming initialization and provides exercises related to weight initialization and BatchNorm folding for inference optimization.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.

videos.title: Building makemore Part 2: MLP
videos.summary: This video, part two of the 'makemore' series, implements a multilayer perceptron (MLP) character-level language model. It covers fundamental machine learning concepts such as model training, learning rate tuning, hyperparameters, evaluation, train/dev/test splits, and under/overfitting. The tutorial walks through building the network components, including the embedding lookup table, hidden layer, output layer, and negative log likelihood loss, using PyTorch. It also discusses training loops, minibatches, and visualizing character embeddings, concluding with sampling from the trained model.
videos.transcript_error: Used description as fallback because captions and Whisper failed.
